title: Girnar Jain temples
tagline: Girnar Jain temples — spiritual town of Gujarat

overview.short: There is a group of temples of Jainism on Mount Girnar near Junagadh in Junagadh district, Gujarat, India.
overview.description: There is a group of temples of Jainism on Mount Girnar near Junagadh in Junagadh district, Gujarat, India. From the 14 temples that are on the hill about 3500 to 4000 steps from bhavnathi taleti and sahesavan, diksha and kevalgyan kalyanak bhumi and nirvan kalyanak bhumi most all the temples belong to the Śvetāmbara sect, only one or two belong to the Digambara sect. The hill and some of the temples are considered sacred by both Digambara and the Śvetāmbara branches of Jainism.

topPlaces.name: Neminath Jain Temple, Girnar
topPlaces.description: Neminath Jain Temple, originally known as Karnavihara, is a Jain temple dedicated to the Jain tirthankara Neminatha. It is on Girnar hill, near the city of Junagadh in Gujarat, India.

topPlaces.name: Ambika Temple, Girnar
topPlaces.description: Ambika Temple, also known as Ambaji Temple, is a temple dedicated to Jain goddess Ambika on Girnar hill in Junagadh, Gujarat, India. The earliest mention of the temple is recorded in the 8th century.

topPlaces.name: Girnar ropeway
topPlaces.description: Girnar ropeway is a ropeway on Mount Girnar in Junagadh district, Gujarat, India. First proposed in 1983, the construction started only in September 2018 due to government approval delays and litigation.

topPlaces.name: Damodar Kund
topPlaces.description: Damodar Kund, located at the foothills of Girnar hills, near Junagadh in the Indian state of Gujarat, is considered a sacred lake as per Hindu beliefs. Many Hindus prefer to bathe here and immerse the ashes and the bones left after cremation of dead bodies, here at Damodar Kund due to a belief that the departed souls would get moksha here.

topPlaces.name: Girnar
topPlaces.description: Girnar is an ancient hill in Junagadh, Gujarat, India. It is one of the holiest pilgrimages of Jains, a Mahatirth, where the 22nd Tirthaṅkar, Lord Neminath attained omniscience, and later nirvana at its highest peak (Neminath Shikhar), along with other five hundred and thirty three enlightened sages.

topPlaces.name: Willingdon Dam
topPlaces.description: Willingdon Dam is situated to the east of the town of Junagadh, near the foot of Datar hills, in Gujarat State, and built on the Kalwa River in India. The dam was named after Marquess Willingdon, the Governor of India at that time.

topPlaces.name: Adi Kadi Vav
topPlaces.description: Adi Kadi Vav or Adi Chadi Vav is a stepwell in Uparkot Fort, Junagadh, Gujarat, India. The date of its construction is uncertain.

topPlaces.name: Jumma Masjid, Uparkot
topPlaces.description: The Jumma Masjid or Jama Masjid is a former Friday mosque, now in partial ruins, in Uparkot Fort in Junagadh, in the state of Gujarat, India. The mosque was built in 15th century by converting a temple or a palace identified as Ranakdevi Mahal by local people and the Archaeological Survey of India.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Neminath Jain Temple, Girnar & around

items.note: Neminath Jain Temple, originally known as Karnavihara, is a Jain temple dedicated to the Jain tirthankara Neminatha. It is on Girnar hill, near the city of Junagadh in Gujarat, India.
Ambika Temple, also known as Ambaji Temple, is a temple dedicated to Jain goddess Ambika on Girnar hill in Junagadh, Gujarat, India. The earliest mention of the temple is recorded in the 8th century.

Girnar ropeway is a ropeway on Mount Girnar in Junagadh district, Gujarat, India. First proposed in 1983, the construction started only in September 2018 due to government approval delays and litigation.

Damodar Kund, located at the foothills of Girnar hills, near Junagadh in the Indian state of Gujarat, is considered a sacred lake as per Hindu beliefs. Many Hindus prefer to bathe here and immerse the ashes and the bones left after cremation of dead bodies, here at Damodar Kund due to a belief that the departed souls would get moksha here.

Girnar is an ancient hill in Junagadh, Gujarat, India. It is one of the holiest pilgrimages of Jains, a Mahatirth, where the 22nd Tirthaṅkar, Lord Neminath attained omniscience, and later nirvana at its highest peak (Neminath Shikhar), along with other five hundred and thirty three enlightened sages.

Willingdon Dam is situated to the east of the town of Junagadh, near the foot of Datar hills, in Gujarat State, and built on the Kalwa River in India. The dam was named after Marquess Willingdon, the Governor of India at that time.
Adi Kadi Vav or Adi Chadi Vav is a stepwell in Uparkot Fort, Junagadh, Gujarat, India. The date of its construction is uncertain.

The Jumma Masjid or Jama Masjid is a former Friday mosque, now in partial ruins, in Uparkot Fort in Junagadh, in the state of Gujarat, India. The mosque was built in 15th century by converting a temple or a palace identified as Ranakdevi Mahal by local people and the Archaeological Survey of India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Girnar Jain temples?
faq.a: The best time to visit Girnar Jain temples is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Girnar Jain temples?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Porbandar Airport (90 km) and the nearest railway station is Rajkot Junction (89 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Girnar Jain temples?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Girnar Jain temples at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Girnar Jain temples?
faq.a: Stays in Girnar Jain temples start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Girnar Jain temples?
faq.a: The top attractions are Neminath Jain Temple, Girnar, Ambika Temple, Girnar, Girnar ropeway, Damodar Kund, Girnar.
